text: Artificial intelligence is becoming more common in daily life, from job applications to loan approvals. However, AI systems can sometimes make unfair decisions. One major concern is bias. If an AI is trained on data that contains human biases, it may repeat or even worsen these biases, leading to unfair treatment of certain groups. Fairness means designing AI to treat everyone equally regardless of race, gender, or background. Privacy is also important: AI should not access or share personal data without permission. Transparency requires that users understand how an AI makes its decisions. For responsible AI development, companies must test their systems carefully and involve diverse teams. By focusing on ethics, we can create AI that helps everyone fairly and safely.
